generated_oml:
// C1: System Under Study
instance BuildingDigitalTwinSystem : DTDFVocab:SystemUnderStudy [
    base:desc "A Digital Twin framework for smart buildings integrating static BIM models with dynamic Building Control Systems (BCS) components to enable real-time monitoring and control of building operations."
    base:contains BuildingInformationModel, BuildingControlSystemComponents, PhysicalInfrastructure,
                   TemperatureSensor, OccupancySensor, EnergyMeter, HVACController,
                   HVACActuator, LightingActuator, SecurityActuator
    DTDFVocab:hasEnvironment SmartBuildingEnvironment
]

instance BuildingInformationModel : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Static representation of physical building components (structural, mechanical, electrical systems) serving as foundational input for DT deployment."
    base:isContainedIn SmartBuildingEnvironment
]

instance BuildingControlSystemComponents : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Dynamic elements including sensors, actuators, and controllers enabling real-time monitoring and control of building operations."
    base:isContainedIn SmartBuildingEnvironment
]

instance PhysicalInfrastructure : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Operational building systems (HVAC, lighting, security) managed via the DT by facility managers."
    base:isContainedIn SmartBuildingEnvironment
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance HVACActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Physical actuator for HVAC systems modeled within BIM environment, operated remotely by Facility Managers via DT control interfaces."
    base:isContainedIn SmartBuildingEnvironment
]

instance LightingActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Physical actuator for lighting systems modeled within BIM environment, enabling remote control through DT interfaces."
    base:isContainedIn SmartBuildingEnvironment
]

instance SecurityActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Physical actuator for security systems (e.g., access control, alarms) integrated into BIM models for DT-enabled management."
    base:isContainedIn SmartBuildingEnvironment
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Generic environmental temperature sensor defined within BIM models for real-time building monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureSensorDataTransmitted
    base:isContainedIn SmartBuildingEnvironment
]

instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Occupancy detection sensor modeled in BIM environment for space utilization analytics."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancySensorDataTransmitted
    base:isContainedIn SmartBuildingEnvironment
]

instance EnergyMeter : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Energy consumption monitoring device integrated into BIM models for operational efficiency tracking."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyMeterDataTransmitted
    base:isContainedIn SmartBuildingEnvironment
]

instance HVACController : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Building Control System controller for HVAC operations, modeled within BIM to enable DT integration."
    DTDFVocab:producedData HVACControllerDataTransmitted
    base:isContainedIn SmartBuildingEnvironment
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance TemperatureSensorDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
]

instance OccupancySensorDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance EnergyMeterDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyMeter
]

instance HVACControllerDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HVACController
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance EnrichedBIMModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Semantic-enriched BIM model integrating device definitions (sensors, actuators, controllers) for DT transformation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BIMToDTTransformationEnabler
]

instance GeometricModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Derived from BIM, provides spatial representation for DT visualization and analysis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationEnabler
]

instance BehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Control logic and operational workflows extracted from BIM for DT runtime execution."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ControlLogicEnabler
]

instance RealTimeOperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Continuous stream of sensor readings and actuator states from physical building systems."
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureSensorDataTransmitted, OccupancySensorDataTransmitted, EnergyMeterDataTransmitted, HVACControllerDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataSynchronizationEnabler
]

instance StaticDeviceMetadata : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Device configurations, spatial relationships, and semantic mappings between BIM elements and DT components."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SemanticMappingEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance BIMToDTTransformationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-Driven Engineering framework automating extraction of structured representations from enriched BIM models to generate deployable DT artifacts."
    DTDFVocab:enables DeviceModelingService, ArtifactGenerationService
]

instance VisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "3D rendering and user interaction tools providing immersive monitoring interfaces for Facility Managers."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, InteractiveVisualizationService
]

instance ControlLogicEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Runtime execution environment for behavioral models enabling automated building management."
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlService
]

instance DataSynchronizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Protocol-mediated communication infrastructure ensuring continuous synchronization between physical and digital systems."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeDataExchangeService
]

instance SemanticMappingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Mechanism maintaining semantic relationships between BIM elements and DT components throughout lifecycle."
    DTDFVocab:enables DeviceConfigurationAction
]

instance CustomServiceIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Modular architecture with standardized extension points enabling integration of third-party analytics and plugins."
    DTDFVocab:enables CustomServiceDevelopmentService
]

// C6: Services
instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time monitoring of building operations and environmental conditions through immersive visualization interfaces."
    DTDFVocab:provides BuildingStateInsight, PerformanceMetricsInsight
]

instance ControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Operation of physical infrastructure (HVAC, lighting, security) via DT control interfaces."
    DTDFVocab:provides HVACAdjustmentAction, LightingControlAction
]

instance CustomServiceDevelopmentService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Development and integration of custom services/plugins to extend DT functionality through standardized extension points."
    DTDFVocab:provides PredictiveMaintenanceInsight, EnergyOptimizationInsight
]

instance DeviceModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Definition of sensors, actuators, and controllers within BIM models creating unified semantic foundation."
    DTDFVocab:provides DeviceConfigurationAction
]

instance RealTimeDataExchangeService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous synchronization between physical buildings and their digital counterparts using protocol-mediated communication."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataSynchronizationAction
]

instance ArtifactGenerationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automated generation of deployable DT artifacts (runtime models, service interfaces) from enriched BIM representations."
    DTDFVocab:provides DeploymentArtifactAction
]

instance InteractiveVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Immersive interfaces enabling intuitive exploration of building operations and performance metrics."
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualExplorationAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance BuildingStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time system state monitoring providing Facility Managers with current operational status of building components."
]

instance PerformanceMetricsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantitative building performance metrics derived from sensor data and operational parameters."
]

instance HVACAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated or manual adjustment of HVAC systems based on real-time conditions and control logic."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance LightingControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remote operation of lighting systems through DT interfaces with configurable scenarios."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Prognostic analytics identifying potential equipment failures before they occur."
]

instance EnergyOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Recommendations for energy consumption reduction based on real-time operational data and historical patterns."
]

instance DeviceConfigurationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Definition and configuration of sensors, actuators, and controllers within BIM environment."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance DataSynchronizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Continuous bidirectional data exchange between physical and digital building representations."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DeploymentArtifactAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated generation of runtime models and service interfaces from BIM-derived representations."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance VisualExplorationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Interactive navigation and analysis of building operations through 3D visualization interfaces."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C12: Environment
instance SmartBuildingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Operational context for the Building Digital Twin system including all physical and digital components."
    base:contains BuildingInformationModel, BuildingControlSystemComponents, PhysicalInfrastructure,
                   TemperatureSensor, OccupancySensor, EnergyMeter, HVACController,
                   HVACActuator, LightingActuator, SecurityActuator
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) system enables control flows from the digital to physical domain through a **BIM-centric control configuration framework**, where **Facility Managers** interact with deployed DTs for real-time monitoring and control. Command types are derived from enriched BIM models and operational requirements, with message formats standardized via the framework’s modular architecture. Validation and authentication mechanisms are not explicitly detailed, but the system’s **non-functional requirements (NF_X)** emphasize interoperability and accessibility, suggesting implicit compliance with domain-specific protocols. Feedback systems are implied through bidirectional data exchange, though specific conflict resolution or command execution confirmation protocols are not described. The framework supports **runtime configuration management**, allowing dynamic adjustments to control logic without redeployment."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system in the provided context emphasizes **real-time synchronization** between physical assets and their digital counterparts, enabling **live data integration** for continuous monitoring and analysis. While explicit synchronization frequencies or latency requirements are not specified, the system is designed to support **real-time insights** and **predictive analytics** by leveraging runtime data updates. The temporal granularity is implied to be **high-resolution**, as the system must accommodate dynamic operational adjustments (e.g., refurbishments, adaptations) across the building lifecycle. The architecture relies on **real-time data exchange** mechanisms to maintain alignment between the physical and digital domains, though no specific time scales (e.g., sub-second, hourly) are detailed in the document."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin constellation** in the AECO domain is designed as a **cyber-physical information construct** integrating **static models** (e.g., Building Information Modeling (BIM)) with **runtime data** to enable **simulation, analysis, and optimization**. The orchestration relies on **carefully designed integration patterns** to bridge **fragmented models and data**, addressing the **heterogeneous nature of infrastructure environments**. Key coordination mechanisms include:
  - **Architecture patterns**: Likely **service-oriented or microservices-based** to modularize components (e.g., simulation engines, predictive analytics, real-time monitoring).
  - **Component integration**: Combines **static asset models** (e.g., BIM) with **dynamic runtime data** (e.g., IoT sensor feeds, operational logs) via **data-driven pipelines**.
  - **Resource management**: Prioritizes **real-time data synchronization** and **predictive analytics** to support **decision-making across the building lifecycle** (design, construction, operations, refurbishment).
  - **System-wide coordination**: Requires **interoperability standards** (e.g., IFC, CityGML) and **cross-domain protocols** to unify disparate data sources, though specific technologies are not detailed. The constellation must handle **scalability challenges** due to the **complexity of AECO environments**, where **stakeholder collaboration** (e.g., architects, operators) demands **unified access to insights**."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in the proposed model-driven deployment framework spans from **initial device modeling** to **custom DT service development**, addressing the gap between static BIM representations and dynamic DT requirements. Key phases include:
  1. **Device Modeling and Configuration**: Building Control Engineers define sensors, actuators, and controllers directly within the BIM environment, enriching static BIM models with Building Control System (BCS) components. This phase establishes a **unified semantic foundation** for subsequent transformations.
  2. **Model Transformation and Artifact Generation**: Leveraging **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) principles**, the framework extracts structured representations from enriched BIM models and **automatically generates deployable DT artifacts** with minimal manual intervention.
  3. **DT Deployment and Synchronization**: The framework ensures **real-time data exchange** between physical buildings and their digital counterparts, maintaining continuous synchronization.
  4. **Runtime Operation and Extension**: Facility Managers monitor system states and interact with the DT to operate physical infrastructure, while DT Engineers integrate **custom services and plugins** via standardized extension points, enabling modular evolution.
  Transition management is governed by the framework’s **modular architecture**, which supports seamless integration of new services and analytics while maintaining interoperability. Lifecycle governance is implicit in the **use-case-driven requirements** (e.g., Table II) and the **role-based responsibilities** (e.g., Table I), ensuring alignment with stakeholder needs (Building Control Engineers, Facility Managers, DT Engineers)."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin framework prioritizes **model fidelity** by addressing BIM’s static limitations through **dynamic, enriched representations** of physical components (e.g., sensors, actuators). **Validation and verification** are implicit in the **model-driven deployment process**, where BIM-enriched models undergo **automated transformations** to generate deployable DT artifacts. **Uncertainty sources** may arise from discrepancies between BIM’s static geometry and real-world changes (e.g., renovations), but the framework aims to mitigate this via **continuous synchronization** between physical and digital systems. **Error detection** is enabled through **real-time data exchange**, where deviations between sensor readings and model predictions trigger alerts or corrective actions. **Quality assurance** is ensured by the **semantic consistency** of BIM-derived models and the **modular, extensible architecture**, which allows for **custom validation services** (e.g., rule-based checks, simulation testing). The document does not specify **quantitative fidelity metrics** (e.g., error thresholds) or **formal verification methods**, but the emphasis on **automated transformations** and **runtime synchronization** underpins the system’s reliability."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) system’s technical network infrastructure relies on **Eclipse Hono** and **Ditto** for real-time data exchange, though specific communication protocols (e.g., MQTT, OPC UA) or network topology (e.g., hybrid, edge) are not explicitly named. The framework’s modular architecture supports **standardized extension points**, enabling integration with heterogeneous systems. Bandwidth and latency requirements are inferred to prioritize **continuous synchronization** between physical and digital counterparts, but no quantitative thresholds (e.g., <100ms) are provided. Reliability and redundancy mechanisms are addressed under **non-functional requirements (NF_X)**, ensuring scalability and maintainability. Security protocols for network communication are not detailed beyond the framework’s emphasis on interoperability and accessibility."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT system supports deployment via a **modular framework** with **runtime configuration management**, enabling **low-overhead updates** and **scalability** from individual components to entire infrastructure systems. While the exact hosting platforms (e.g., cloud, edge, on-premise) are not explicitly stated, the system’s **interactive visualization** (e.g., Unreal Engine for immersive interfaces) suggests a hybrid or distributed deployment model. Computational and storage requirements are tailored to **operational needs**, with the framework’s extensibility allowing custom services and analytics integration. Scalability mechanisms are embedded in the **modular architecture**, ensuring adaptability to evolving requirements. Deployment models are not detailed, but the system’s **bidirectional data exchange** and **holistic life cycle management** imply support for **hybrid or edge-cloud deployments**. Availability and reliability are assured through the **non-functional requirements (NF_X)**, which address **maintainability** and **interoperability** across heterogeneous environments."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system addresses **fragmented data sources** and **heterogeneous infrastructure environments** by enabling **real-time data exchange** via **Eclipse Hono** (for IoT/edge data) and **Eclipse Ditto** (for twin state management). Integration with external systems is implied to support **interoperability** but lacks specific protocols or standards (e.g., no mention of IFC, CityGML, OData, or ISO 19650). Dependencies on external systems are not detailed, though the system must interface with **domain-specific tools** (e.g., ERP, CMMS) and **operational data streams** (e.g., sensor networks, weather feeds) to deliver **data-driven infrastructure management**. The document does not specify integration patterns (e.g., API gateways, event-driven architectures) or enterprise system dependencies."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The framework adheres to **semantic standardization** through a **modeling methodology** that unifies BIM semantics with dynamic DT requirements, addressing BIM’s limitations in representing Building Control Systems (BCS). Integration standardization is achieved via **standardized extension points** in the modular architecture, enabling seamless incorporation of custom services and third-party tools. While the document does not explicitly name **industry standards** (e.g., IFC, OPC UA), it implies compliance with **BIM interoperability principles** (e.g., IFC-like semantic enrichment) and **real-time data exchange protocols** (e.g., Hono/Ditto for synchronization). Certification requirements or formal compliance frameworks (e.g., ISO 19650, IEC 62424) are not mentioned, nor are explicit **interoperability standards** beyond the modular architecture’s design. The focus is on **internal standardization** (e.g., unified semantic foundation, extension points) rather than external certification."
]
